title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns; Britain's Seventh PM Since Brexit
summary: Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, becoming the sixth PM to leave office since Brexit in 2016 (making him the departure that sets up the seventh PM post-Brexit). Britain has seen remarkable political instability with multiple prime ministerial changes in a short period. The process for selecting a new UK PM involves a Conservative/Labour party leadership contest under the Westminster parliamentary system. This resignation is significant given India-UK bilateral relations, including the recently concluded India-UK Free Trade Agreement.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (International Relations) — India-UK relations, Westminster model of government, and political developments in major democracies. Also connects to the India-UK FTA context.
key_facts: Keir Starmer resigns as UK Prime Minister in June 2026
Britain has had 7 PMs in roughly 10 years since Brexit (2016)
UK follows Westminster parliamentary model for PM selection
India-UK Free Trade Agreement was recently concluded under Starmer's tenure

title: India's Core Sector Growth Slows to Seven-Month Low of 0.5% in May 2026
summary: India's eight core sector industries recorded a growth of only 0.5% in May 2026, the slowest pace in seven months. The core sector comprises eight key industries — coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilisers, steel, cement, and electricity — which together have a combined weight of about 40.27% in the Index of Industrial Production (IIP). A slowdown in core sector output is a leading indicator of broader industrial deceleration. This data will be closely watched ahead of the IIP release.
exam_angle: Core sector data is a lead indicator for IIP and GDP; understanding its eight components and their IIP weightage is a standard GS Paper 3 economy question.
key_facts: Core sector growth: 0.5% in May 2026 — seven-month low
Eight core industries have ~40.27% weight in IIP
Eight industries: coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilisers, steel, cement, electricity
Core sector data released by Ministry of Commerce & Industry

title: India in Talks to Sell Supersonic BrahMos Missile to UAE; Akashteer Defence System Also Under Discussion
summary: India is in active negotiations to sell the supersonic BrahMos cruise missile to the United Arab Emirates, which would mark another major defence export milestone. Simultaneously, India's Akashteer automated air defence control and reporting system is also reportedly part of defence discussions with the UAE. BrahMos is a joint venture between India's DRDO and Russia's NPO Mashinostroyeniya, capable of speeds up to Mach 2.8–3. India had previously concluded BrahMos deals with the Philippines and other nations as part of its push to become a top defence exporter.
exam_angle: India's defence exports target of USD 5 billion by 2025 and the role of BrahMos as a flagship export product are recurring GS Paper 3 (Defence/Economy) and GS Paper 2 (IR) topics.
key_facts: BrahMos: India-Russia joint venture (DRDO + NPO Mashinostroyeniya)
Speed: Mach 2.8–3; supersonic cruise missile
Akashteer: India's automated air defence control & reporting system
India previously sold BrahMos to Philippines; UAE deal under negotiation

title: NSA Ajit Doval Holds Talks with Iran's Top Security Official Amid Middle East Tensions
summary: National Security Adviser Ajit Doval held diplomatic talks with Iran's top security official amid escalating Middle East tensions, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's threat to restrict shipping through the Strait of Hormuz — through which a significant share of global oil trade passes — has heightened geopolitical concerns. India imports a notable portion of its crude oil from the Gulf region, making stability in the Strait of Hormuz critical to India's energy security. The talks underline India's strategic engagement with Iran on security and energy matters.
exam_angle: Strait of Hormuz is critical for India's energy security (oil imports); NSA-level diplomacy with Iran is relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) — India-Iran relations, India's energy security, and West Asia policy.
key_facts: NSA Ajit Doval met Iran's top security official in June 2026
Strait of Hormuz: ~20% of global oil trade passes through it
India is a major importer of Gulf crude oil
Context: Iran-US nuclear deal talks and Middle East tensions

title: US Eases Sanctions on Iranian Oil Exports Amid Finalisation of Nuclear Peace Deal
summary: The United States has authorised Iranian oil sales and eased related sanctions as negotiations on a final nuclear peace agreement with Iran move towards conclusion. US Vice President JD Vance stated that the US would ensure any unfrozen Iranian funds would not finance terrorism. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to visit Gulf countries to engage regional stakeholders. The easing of Iranian oil sanctions could affect global crude oil prices and India's energy import calculations.
exam_angle: Iran nuclear deal negotiations and US sanctions policy directly impact global oil prices and India's energy security — relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Energy Security).
key_facts: US authorised Iranian oil sales amid nuclear deal talks (June 2026)
JD Vance: US to ensure unfrozen Iranian funds won't finance terrorism
Marco Rubio (US Sec of State) to visit Gulf countries
Easing sanctions could lower global crude oil prices

title: India Becomes World's Top Ship Recycling Nation in 2025, Achieves Vision 2030 Target Five Years Ahead of Schedule
summary: India has surpassed all other nations to become the world's leading ship recycling country in 2025, achieving its Vision 2030 target five years ahead of schedule. The Alang ship recycling yard in Gujarat is the largest such facility in the world and the hub of India's ship recycling industry. India accounts for over 30% of global ship recycling by gross tonnage. This achievement reflects India's growing maritime sector and its compliance with the Hong Kong Convention for safe and environmentally sound ship recycling.
exam_angle: India's maritime sector milestones, Sagarmala Project, and the Hong Kong Convention on ship recycling are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy/Infrastructure) and GS Paper 2 (IR/Environment).
key_facts: India ranked No. 1 in global ship recycling in 2025
Vision 2030 ship recycling target achieved 5 years early
Alang (Gujarat): world's largest ship recycling yard
India accounts for >30% of global ship recycling by gross tonnage

title: India Imposes Anti-Dumping Duty on Rubber Chemical Imports from China, EU, and US
summary: India has slapped anti-dumping duties on imports of certain rubber chemicals from China, the European Union, and the United States. Anti-dumping duties are levied under WTO rules when imported goods are found to be sold below their normal value, causing material injury to the domestic industry. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR) under the Ministry of Commerce recommends such duties to protect domestic manufacturers. This measure aims to protect India's domestic rubber chemicals industry from unfair import competition.
exam_angle: Anti-dumping duties, the role of DGTR, and WTO's Anti-Dumping Agreement are standard GS Paper 3 (Economy/Trade) topics; also relevant to India's industrial policy.
key_facts: Anti-dumping duty imposed on rubber chemicals from China, EU, and US
Recommended by Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR), Ministry of Commerce
WTO's Anti-Dumping Agreement (Article VI of GATT) governs such measures
Purpose: protect domestic rubber chemical industry from below-cost imports

title: India Seeks Preferential Tariff Access in Push to Finalise US Trade Deal
summary: India is actively seeking preferential tariff access — an advantage over competing nations — as negotiations to finalise a bilateral trade agreement with the United States intensify. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal stated India is 'trying to work out' the deal as fast as possible to gain a competitive edge. India wants tariff benefits better than those given to peer economies in the context of US reciprocal tariff policies announced in 2025. A bilateral trade deal with the US is crucial for India given the US being India's largest trading partner.
exam_angle: India-US trade negotiations, reciprocal tariffs, and India's trade diplomacy are important for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Trade). Piyush Goyal's role as Commerce Minister is relevant for Persons in News.
key_facts: India seeking preferential tariff access in India-US bilateral trade deal
Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal leading negotiations
US is India's largest trading partner
Context: US reciprocal tariff regime announced in 2025

title: Supreme Court Rules Right to Walk on Footpaths is a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on footpaths constitutes a fundamental right of citizens, expanding the scope of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) to include the right to safe pedestrian infrastructure. The court emphasised that encroachments on footpaths by vendors, vehicles, or constructions violate citizens' fundamental rights. This ruling has significant implications for urban local bodies and their obligation to maintain clear pedestrian pathways. It builds on previous SC jurisprudence expanding Article 21 to include quality-of-life rights.
exam_angle: Expansive interpretation of Article 21 by the Supreme Court is a recurring theme in GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance); this ruling connects to urban governance, right to life, and Directive Principles on living standards.
key_facts: SC ruled: right to walk on footpaths = fundamental right
Constitutional basis: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Encroachments on footpaths declared violative of fundamental rights
Ruling imposes obligation on urban local bodies to maintain pedestrian infrastructure

title: Supreme Court Refuses to Entertain Himachal Pradesh Judicial Officer's Plea Against Collegium Picks for HC Judgeship
summary: The Supreme Court refused to entertain a plea filed by a Himachal Pradesh judicial officer challenging the Supreme Court Collegium's recommendations for appointment to the High Court judgeship. The Collegium system, established through the Second, Third, and Fourth Judges Cases (1993, 1998), gives primacy to the Chief Justice and senior judges in judicial appointments. The SC's refusal reinforces the Collegium's autonomy in making judicial appointments without interference from aspirants or the executive. This is significant in the ongoing debate over judicial appointments and the proposed National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC).
exam_angle: The Collegium system for judicial appointments is a critical GS Paper 2 (Polity) topic — connects to Articles 124, 217, 124A (NJAC — struck down), and the debate on judicial independence vs. accountability.
key_facts: SC refused plea challenging Collegium's HC appointment recommendations
Collegium system established via Second (1993) & Third (1998) Judges Cases
NJAC (Article 124A) was struck down by SC in 2015
Collegium comprises CJI + 4 senior-most SC judges for HC/SC appointments

title: Kerala High Court Upholds Free Bus Travel Scheme for Women and Transgender Persons
summary: The Kerala High Court has upheld the state government's scheme providing free bus travel to women and transgender persons on Kerala State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC) buses. The court held that the scheme is a valid exercise of the state's power to implement affirmative welfare measures and does not violate constitutional provisions. Kerala's scheme is part of broader welfare initiatives targeting gender equity in public transport. Similar free travel schemes for women have been introduced in several states including Delhi and Tamil Nadu.
exam_angle: State welfare schemes related to gender equity, constitutional validity of affirmative measures under Articles 15(3) and 46, and transgender rights (Transgender Persons Act 2019) are relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity/Social Justice).
key_facts: Kerala HC upheld free bus travel scheme for women and transgender persons
Scheme implemented on KSRTC (Kerala State Road Transport Corporation) buses
Constitutional basis: Article 15(3) — special provision for women and children
Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019 provides legal backing for trans inclusion

title: Kunal Shah, Founder of CRED, Appointed as Head of WhatsApp; Meta Invests $900 Million in CRED
summary: Meta has appointed Kunal Shah, the Indian entrepreneur and founder of the fintech startup CRED, to lead WhatsApp globally, replacing Will Cathcart. Simultaneously, Meta announced a $900 million investment in CRED, marking one of the largest foreign investments in an Indian startup. Kunal Shah founded CRED in 2018 as a credit card bill payment and rewards platform; it achieved unicorn status in 2021. This appointment makes Kunal Shah one of the highest-profile Indians leading a global technology platform.
exam_angle: Persons in News (prominent Indian entrepreneurs in global roles) and India's startup ecosystem (unicorns, FDI in tech) are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy/Sci-Tech) and current affairs.
key_facts: Kunal Shah (CRED founder) appointed as head of WhatsApp globally
Meta investing $900 million in CRED (June 2026)
CRED founded in 2018; achieved unicorn status in 2021
Will Cathcart steps back as WhatsApp head

title: Tata Electronics Hit by Cyber Breach; Apple and Tesla Trade Secrets Allegedly Exposed
summary: Tata Electronics, a major contract manufacturer for Apple (iPhones) and Tesla components in India, has reportedly suffered a significant cyber breach with hackers claiming to have exposed sensitive trade secrets of both Apple and Tesla. Tata Electronics operates large manufacturing facilities in Tamil Nadu and Karnataka, and is a key player in India's electronics manufacturing ecosystem under the PLI (Production Linked Incentive) scheme. The breach highlights the cybersecurity vulnerabilities in global supply chains and critical manufacturing infrastructure. India's IT Act 2000 and CERT-In regulations govern cyber incident reporting.
exam_angle: Cybersecurity of critical infrastructure, India's role in global electronics supply chains (PLI scheme), CERT-In regulations, and data protection (DPDP Act 2023) are relevant GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech/Economy) themes.
key_facts: Tata Electronics suffered a cyber breach exposing Apple and Tesla trade secrets
Tata Electronics: major iPhone and Tesla component manufacturer in India
India's CERT-In mandates cyber incident reporting within 6 hours
Tata Electronics operates under PLI scheme for electronics manufacturing

title: Five Eyes Intelligence Alliance Warns of AI Models Capable of Destabilising Governments Within Months
summary: In a rare joint statement, the Five Eyes intelligence alliance (comprising USA, UK, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand) warned that AI models with the capability to take down governments and businesses could emerge within months. The statement highlights the dual-use risks of frontier AI systems and calls for coordinated international governance of advanced AI development. This comes amid rapid advancements by companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google DeepMind. A US Senator also separately claimed that Anthropic's AI model had breached nearly all NSA systems in a security test.
exam_angle: AI governance, dual-use technology risks, and international frameworks for AI regulation are increasingly important for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) and GS Paper 2 (IR — multilateral governance).
key_facts: Five Eyes = US, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand intelligence alliance
Warning: frontier AI models could destabilise governments within months
US Senator claimed Anthropic's AI breached nearly all NSA systems in a test
Calls for international AI governance frameworks intensifying

title: India Needs Indigenous AI Model to Avoid Dependence on US AI: Bernstein Report
summary: A report by financial research firm Bernstein warns that India risks becoming overly dependent on US AI models (such as those from OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic) and needs to develop its own sovereign AI model — akin to China's DeepSeek — to ensure technological autonomy. India's National AI Mission and IndiaAI initiative have been launched to build domestic AI capabilities, but significant gaps remain. The report notes that without a domestic foundation model, India's AI stack and data sovereignty could be compromised. This aligns with broader debates about AI sovereignty and digital colonisation.
exam_angle: India's AI policy (IndiaAI Mission, National AI Strategy), digital sovereignty, and the geopolitics of AI are important for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) and GS Paper 2 (IR/Governance).
key_facts: Bernstein report: India needs its own AI foundation model like China's DeepSeek
India's IndiaAI Mission and National AI Strategy aim to build domestic AI capacity
Risk: AI dependence on US firms could compromise data sovereignty
China's DeepSeek is cited as a model for sovereign open-weight AI development

title: Mahrang Baloch, Prominent Baloch Human Rights Activist, Sentenced to Life Imprisonment by Pakistan
summary: Mahrang Baloch, a prominent Pakistani Baloch human rights activist and leader of the Baloch Yakjehti Committee, has been sentenced to life imprisonment by a Pakistani court. Mahrang Baloch had been a vocal campaigner against enforced disappearances and extrajudicial killings in Balochistan and was internationally recognised for her activism, including being named among TIME magazine's influential persons. Her sentencing has drawn sharp criticism from international human rights organisations. The Balochistan issue has implications for India-Pakistan relations and regional security.
exam_angle: Human rights in Pakistan's Balochistan, India-Pakistan relations, and the broader issue of enforced disappearances as a human rights concern are relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR/Governance) and current affairs.
key_facts: Mahrang Baloch: leader of Baloch Yakjehti Committee, Pakistan
Sentenced to life imprisonment by Pakistani court (June 2026)
Campaigned against enforced disappearances and extrajudicial killings in Balochistan
Named in TIME magazine's list of influential persons previously

title: Argentina Eyes Larger Role in India's Energy Security as Bilateral Trade Crosses $5 Billion
summary: Argentina is seeking to expand its role in India's energy security as bilateral trade between the two countries has crossed the $5 billion mark. Argentina, a major producer of lithium (critical for EV batteries), shale oil, and agricultural commodities, is positioning itself as a strategic energy and resources partner for India. India's Critical Mineral Mission identifies lithium as a priority mineral, and Argentina is part of the 'Lithium Triangle' (along with Chile and Bolivia) that holds the world's largest lithium reserves. Enhanced India-Argentina ties also have implications for India's Global South engagement.
exam_angle: Critical minerals (lithium triangle), India's energy security, and India-Latin America relations are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy/Environment) and GS Paper 2 (IR — India's foreign policy with Global South).
key_facts: India-Argentina bilateral trade crossed $5 billion
Argentina: part of 'Lithium Triangle' (Argentina, Chile, Bolivia) — world's largest lithium reserves
India's Critical Mineral Mission lists lithium as a priority
Argentina also a major shale oil and agricultural commodities producer

title: Israeli Defence Delegation Visits India to Deepen Strategic Partnership
summary: An Israeli defence delegation visited India to strengthen the two countries' strategic and defence partnership, which covers areas including missile defence, drone technology, cybersecurity, and intelligence sharing. India-Israel defence ties have grown significantly since the normalisation of diplomatic relations in 1992 and especially after Prime Minister Modi's visit to Israel in 2017 — the first ever by an Indian PM. Israel is one of India's top three defence suppliers. The visit comes amid heightened Middle East tensions following regional conflicts.
exam_angle: India-Israel defence and strategic relations, India's defence imports and the push for Atmanirbhar Bharat in defence are standard GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Defence) topics.
key_facts: Israeli defence delegation visited India (June 2026) to deepen strategic ties
India-Israel diplomatic relations established in 1992
PM Modi's Israel visit in 2017: first ever by an Indian PM
Israel is among India's top three defence suppliers (along with Russia and France)

title: Supreme Court Plea Seeks FIR and CBI Probe into Alleged Ram Temple Fund Irregularities
summary: A plea has been filed in the Supreme Court of India seeking registration of an FIR and a CBI-led investigation into alleged financial irregularities in the funds collected for the Ram Temple construction in Ayodhya. The Ram Temple (Ram Mandir) at Ayodhya was consecrated in January 2024 and is managed by the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. The SC will examine whether such oversight falls within its jurisdiction or is better addressed through other legal mechanisms. This is significant given the constitutional and legal sensitivities around the Ayodhya matter.
exam_angle: Ram Mandir case history, the role of the SC in overseeing public trusts, and CBI jurisdiction are relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance). Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust is key institutional knowledge.
key_facts: Plea in SC seeks FIR + CBI probe into Ram Temple fund irregularities
Ram Temple consecrated: January 22, 2024
Managing body: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust
SC to decide on jurisdiction and admissibility of the plea

title: Tamil Nadu Ranks Second in India in Hot-Humid Days: Climate Report
summary: Tamil Nadu has been ranked second among Indian states in terms of the number of hot-humid days, according to a recent climate study. Hot-humid conditions (combining high temperature with high humidity) pose greater health risks than dry heat as they impair the body's ability to cool through perspiration. India's vulnerability to extreme heat events has been increasing due to climate change, with the Heat Action Plans being implemented in several states. Tamil Nadu's coastal geography and tropical climate contribute to its high incidence of such conditions.
exam_angle: Climate change impacts on India, Heat Action Plans, wet-bulb temperature and human health, and India's vulnerability to extreme weather events are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Environment) and GS Paper 1 (Physical Geography).
key_facts: Tamil Nadu ranked 2nd in India for hot-humid days (2026 climate report)
Hot-humid conditions measured by wet-bulb temperature (combines heat + humidity)
India has Heat Action Plans (HAPs) in several states to mitigate heat risks
Climate change is increasing frequency and intensity of hot-humid days across India
